section.heading: Improper claim for Trump account contribution pilot program credit

subsection.heading: In general
subsection.chapeau: In the case of any individual who makes an election under section 6434 with respect to an individual who is not an eligible child of the taxpayer—

paragraph.content: if such election was made due to negligence or disregard of the rules or regulations, there shall be imposed a penalty of $500, or
if such election was made due to fraud, there shall be imposed a penalty of $1,000.

content.p: The term “eligible child” has the meaning given such term under section 6434.
The terms “negligence” and “disregard” have the same meaning as when such terms are used in section 6662.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 119–21, title VII, § 70204(d)(2), July 4, 2025, 139 Stat. 188.)

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 6659, added Pub. L. 97–34, title VII, § 722(a)(1), Aug. 13, 1981, 95 Stat. 341; amended Pub. L. 97–448, title I, § 107(a)(1), (2), Jan. 12, 1983, 96 Stat. 2391; Pub. L. 98–369, div. A, title I, § 155(c)(1), title VII, § 721(x)(4), July 18, 1984, 98 Stat. 693, 971, related to additions to tax in case of valuation overstatements for purposes of the income tax, prior to repeal by Pub. L. 101–239, title VII, § 7721(c)(2), Dec. 19, 1989, 103 Stat. 2399.
Another prior section 6659 was renumbered section 6662 of this title.

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section applicable to taxable years beginning after Dec. 31, 2025, see section 70204(e) of Pub. L. 119–21, set out as a note under section 128 of this title.
